title: Dissipative Time Quasicrystals from Multilevel Interference
authors: Kang Shen, Xiangming Hu, Fei Wang
abstract: Boundary time crystals exhibit spontaneous breaking of continuous time-translation symmetry through persistent periodic oscillations in driven-dissipative many-body systems. Here, we show that multilevel interference provides a natural route beyond periodic order, enabling dissipative time quasicrystals without externally imposed quasiperiodic driving. We consider a collectively driven-dissipative four-level ensemble with two degenerate excited states and two degenerate ground states. In the thermodynamic limit, the exact mean-field dynamics reduces to an irrational flow on a two-dimensional torus, yielding quasiperiodic order parameters with discrete spectra generated by two incommensurate fundamental frequencies. Vanishing maximal Lyapunov exponents demonstrate that the nonlinear self-consistent dynamics remains nonchaotic. Our results establish a minimal interference-induced mechanism for time-quasiperiodic order and open a route toward higher-dimensional quasiperiodic dynamics in multilevel systems.
summary: This paper shows that multilevel interference in a driven-dissipative system can spontaneously break time-translation symmetry to produce quasiperiodic motion. By using a four-level model, the authors demonstrate that an irrational ratio between transition branches leads to a time quasicrystal without needing external quasiperiodic driving. The dynamics are shown to be nonchaotic and characterized by a dense frequency spectrum.
main_problem: Investigating whether dissipative time-crystalline order can extend beyond periodic oscillations to achieve intrinsic quasiperiodic temporal order without external quasiperiodic driving.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that multilevel interference in a driven-dissipative ensemble can generate dissipative time quasicrystals, characterized by an irrational flow on a 2D torus and a dense frequency spectrum.
method: The study employs mean-field theory in the thermodynamic limit, mapping the many-body dynamics to a scalar phase equation and using Fourier analysis and elliptic integrals to derive the frequency spectrum.
model_or_system: A collectively driven-dissipative four-level ensemble of N identical atoms with two degenerate excited and two degenerate ground states, governed by a master equation with multiple transition branches.
key_observables: Collective Bloch vectors, order parameters (m_z1 and m_z1 + m_z2), scalar phase angle theta(t), and maximal Lyapunov exponents.
important_parameters: Coherent driving strength (omega), collective dissipation rate (kappa), and the irrational relative weight of the second transition branch (eta).
main_assumptions: Thermodynamic limit (N to infinity), neglect of 1/N quantum fluctuations, and the assumption of a Diophantine condition for the irrationality of eta.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the trajectory of the Bloch vector, torus-periodic functions, and quasiperiodic signals; Figure 2 compares mean-field vs. finite-N dynamics, shows 2D Fourier amplitudes, and displays the decay of Lyapunov exponents.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the concept of time quasicrystals, defines the four-level driven-dissipative model, derives the mean-field reduction to a phase equation, analyzes the resulting quasiperiodic dynamics and frequency spectrum, and concludes with the stability and generalization of the mechanism.
why_interesting: It provides a novel mechanism for generating complex temporal order (quasiperiodicity) through internal interference rather than external modulation, which is highly relevant for controlling non-equilibrium many-body dynamics and open quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
